383	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
384	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
385	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
386	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
387	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
388	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
389	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
390	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
391	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
392	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
393	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
394	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
395	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
396	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
397	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
398	  map_smul' r s := by
399	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
400	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
401	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
402	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
403	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
404	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
405	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
406	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
407	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
408	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
409	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
410	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
411	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
412	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
413	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
414	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
415	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
416	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
417	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
418	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
419	      intro z
420	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
421	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
422	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
423	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
424	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
425	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
426	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
427	
428	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
429	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
430	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
431	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
432	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
433	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
434	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
435	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
436	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
437	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
438	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
439	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
440	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
441	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
442	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
443	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
444	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
445	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
446	
447	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
448	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
449	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
450	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
451	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
452	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
453	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
454	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
455	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
456	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
457	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
458	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
459	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
460	
461	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
462	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
463	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
464	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
465	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
466	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
467	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
468	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
469	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
470	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
471	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
472	
473	end StalkLinearMap
474	
475	end PresheafOfModules
476	
477	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site

1900	  \((A \otimes^{p} B)(U) = A(U) \otimes_{R(U)} B(U)\)
1901	  (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj\_obj}\)). The neighbourhood system
1902	  \(\{U \ni x\}\) is cofiltered under inclusion, so the colimit is filtered. The
1903	  isomorphism is assembled in five named stages. Stages (i)--(ii) construct the
1904	  forward additive comparison map and its germ characterisation; stages (iii)--(v)
1905	  upgrade it to an \(R_x\)-linear map, construct the reverse map, and check mutual
1906	  inversion.
1907	
1908	  \emph{(i) Per-neighbourhood balanced bilinear map and its section-level descent}
1909	  (\(\mathtt{stalkTensorBilin}\), \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDescU}\)). For a fixed
1910	  neighbourhood \(U \ni x\) the assignment
1911	  \[
1912	    (a, b) \;\longmapsto\; \mathrm{germ}_x\,a \,\otimes\, \mathrm{germ}_x\,b,
1913	    \qquad a \in A(U),\ b \in B(U),
1914	  \]
1915	  is \(R(U)\)-balanced bilinear into \(A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x\): it is additive in each
1916	  variable, and for \(r \in R(U)\) the two ways of moving \(r\) across the tensor agree
1917	  after passing to germs, since \(\mathrm{germ}_x(r \cdot a) = (\mathrm{germ}_x r)
1918	  \cdot \mathrm{germ}_x a\) and the stalk tensor is balanced over
1919	  \(R_x = R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\) (the germ--scalar compatibility of
1920	  \cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}). By the universal property of the relative tensor
1921	  product it descends to an additive map on the section module
1922	  \[
1923	    \mathtt{stalkTensorDescU}_U \;\colon\;
1924	      (A \otimes^{p} B)(U) = A(U) \otimes_{R(U)} B(U)
1925	        \;\longrightarrow\; A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x,
1926	    \qquad a \otimes b \longmapsto \mathrm{germ}_x a \otimes \mathrm{germ}_x b.
1927	  \]
1928	
1929	  \emph{(ii) Colimit descent to the forward comparison map}
1930	  (\(\mathtt{stalkTensorDesc}\), \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDesc\_germ\_tmul}\)). The
1931	  maps \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDescU}_U\) are compatible with the restriction maps of the
1932	  presheaf \(A \otimes^{p} B\) as \(U\) shrinks toward \(x\) (a smaller neighbourhood
1933	  computes the same germs), so they form a cocone over the filtered diagram
1934	  \(\{(A \otimes^{p} B)(U)\}_{U \ni x}\). The induced map out of the colimit is the
1935	  forward additive comparison map
1936	  \[
1937	    \mathtt{stalkTensorDesc} \;\colon\;
1938	      (A \otimes^{p} B).\mathtt{stalk}\,x \;\longrightarrow\; A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x,
1939	  \]
1940	  characterised on germs of simple tensors by
1941	  \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDesc}\bigl(\mathrm{germ}_x(a \otimes b)\bigr) =
1942	  \mathrm{germ}_x a \otimes \mathrm{germ}_x b\)
1943	  (\(\mathtt{stalkTensorDesc\_germ\_tmul}\)). This is the forward half of the asserted
1944	  isomorphism (\cref{lem:stalk_tensor_desc_forward}).
1945	
1946	  \emph{(iii) \(R_x\)-linearity packaging} (\(\mathtt{stalkTensorLinearMap}\)). The
1947	  additive map of (ii) is upgraded to an \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear map: on a germ
1948	  \(\mathrm{germ}_x r\) of a section \(r \in R(U)\) one checks
1949	  \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDesc}\bigl((\mathrm{germ}_x r)\cdot \xi\bigr) =
1950	  (\mathrm{germ}_x r)\cdot \mathtt{stalkTensorDesc}(\xi)\) on germs of simple tensors
1951	  and extends by additivity. The one subtlety is a \emph{carrier-duality obstacle}:
1952	  the section-level tensor \(A(U) \otimes_{R(U)} B(U)\) is a module over the
1953	  \(\mathtt{RingCat}\) carrier \((R \circ \mathtt{forget_2})(U)\), whereas the natural
1954	  scalar \(r : R(U)\) lives in the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) carrier \(R(U)\); the two
1955	  carriers are canonically identified but not definitionally equal, so the
1956	  scalar-pulling lemma \(\mathtt{TensorProduct.smul\_tmul'}\) applies only after
1957	  inserting a canonical \(\mathtt{RingEquiv}\) bridge between the
1958	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and \(\mathtt{RingCat}\) carriers. Once that bridge is
1959	  in place the linearity computation mirrors the germ-representative pattern used
1960	  for the d.1 stalk map \(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\) (\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}).
1961	
1962	  \emph{(iv) The reverse map.} The map
1963	  \[
1964	    A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x \;\longrightarrow\; (A \otimes^{p} B).\mathtt{stalk}\,x
1965	  \]
1966	  is built by the universal property of the tensor product over \(R_x\) from the
1967	  \(R_x\)-bilinear map of the two stalks
1968	  \[
1969	    (\mathrm{germ}_x a,\ \mathrm{germ}_x b) \;\longmapsto\;
1970	      \mathrm{germ}_x\bigl(a|_{U \cap V} \otimes b|_{U \cap V}\bigr),
1971	  \]
1972	  where \(a \in A(U)\) and \(b \in B(V)\) are chosen representatives; this is a
1973	  \emph{nested} colimit descent, descending first the \(A\)-variable out of the
1974	  filtered colimit \(\{A(U)\}_{U \ni x}\) and then the \(B\)-variable out of
1975	  \(\{B(V)\}_{V \ni x}\), passing to the common refinement \(U \cap V\) to form the
1976	  simple tensor and its germ. Well-definedness on germs (independence of the chosen
1977	  representatives and of the neighbourhoods \(U, V\)) is exactly filteredness of the
1978	  neighbourhood system, and \(R_x\)-bilinearity reduces to the germ--scalar
1979	  compatibility of (i)/(iii).
1980	
1981	  \emph{(v) Mutual inversion on germ generators} (\(\mathtt{stalkTensorIso}\)). The two
1982	  composites are checked to be identities on generators. The composite
1983	  \(A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x \to (A \otimes^{p} B).\mathtt{stalk}\,x \to A_x
1984	  \otimes_{R_x} B_x\) is the identity on a simple tensor \(\mathrm{germ}_x a \otimes
1985	  \mathrm{germ}_x b\) by the germ characterisation \(\mathtt{stalkTensorDesc\_germ\_tmul}\)
1986	  of (ii), and extends to all of \(A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x\) by
1987	  \(\mathtt{TensorProduct.induction\_on}\). The opposite composite
1988	  \((A \otimes^{p} B).\mathtt{stalk}\,x \to A_x \otimes_{R_x} B_x \to (A \otimes^{p}
1989	  B).\mathtt{stalk}\,x\) is the identity on a germ \(\mathrm{germ}_x(a \otimes b)\) of
1990	  a simple tensor by the same characterisation; since germs generate the stalk and the
1991	  germ maps are jointly epimorphic (\(\mathtt{stalk\_hom\_ext}\)), together with
1992	  \(\mathtt{TensorProduct.induction\_on}\) applied to each section, the composite is the
1993	  identity. Bundling the \(R_x\)-linear forward map of (iii) with the reverse map of
1994	  (iv) and these two inversion identities yields the asserted isomorphism
1995	  \(\mathtt{stalkTensorIso}\).
1996	
1997	  Naturality in \(A, B\) is inherited from naturality of the sectionwise tensor and of
1998	  the germ maps. This is the presheaf-of-modules, varying-ring analogue of the Stacks
1999	  lemma above; the only subtlety beyond the ringed-space statement is that the ground
2000	  ring varies with \(U\), handled by the ``tensor of filtered colimits over the colimit
2001	  ring'' identification together with the carrier-duality bridge of (iii). Conceptually
2002	  the tensor product of modules commutes with filtered colimits in each variable and
2003	  the ground ring \(R(U)\) itself colimits to the stalk ring \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\),
2004	  which is the reason the forward map (ii) and the reverse map (iv) are mutually
2005	  inverse.
2006	\end{proof}
2007	
2008	\begin{lemma}
2009	[Forward stalk--tensor comparison map (d.2, partial)]
2010	  \label{lem:stalk_tensor_desc_forward}
2011	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorDesc}
2012	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2013	  With \(R\), \(A\), \(B\), \(x\) as in \cref{lem:stalk_tensor_commutation}, there is
2014	  a canonical natural \emph{additive} comparison map
2015	  \[
2016	    \mathtt{stalkTensorDesc} \;\colon\;
2017	      (A \otimes^{p} B).\mathtt{stalk}\,x \;\longrightarrow\;
2018	      A.\mathtt{stalk}\,x \;\otimes_{R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x}\; B.\mathtt{stalk}\,x,
2019	  \]

/-- **The d.2 comparison map** `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`, obtained by descending
the per-neighbourhood maps `stalkTensorDescU` through the colimit defining the stalk. -/
input.new_string:
/-- **Scalar compatibility of the per-neighbourhood descent.** For `r : R(U)` and a
section `z` of `A ⊗ᵖ B` over `U`, the descent `stalkTensorDescU` carries the `R(U)`-scalar
action over to the `R.stalk x`-action by the germ of `r`. This is stage (iii)'s
section-level core; the carrier-duality between the `CommRingCat` scalar `r : R(U)` and the
`RingCat`-carrier module structure on `A(U) ⊗ B(U)` is absorbed by `germ_smul`/`smul_tmul'`. -/
lemma stalkTensorDescU_smul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (r : ↑(R.obj (op U)))
    (z : ↑((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).obj (op U))) :
    (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) (r • z)
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ R U x hx)) r •
          (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) z := by
  induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
  | zero => simp
  | tmul a b =>
      rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', stalkTensorDescU_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul,
        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul A x U hx]
      exact (TensorProduct.smul_tmul' _ _ _).symm
  | add p q hp hq =>
      rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, hp, hq, smul_add]

/-- **The d.2 comparison map** `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`, obtained by descending
the per-neighbourhood maps `stalkTensorDescU` through the colimit defining the stalk. -/

/-- **Element form of `germ_stalkTensorDesc`.** The comparison map sends the germ of a
section `w` over `U` to its per-neighbourhood descent `stalkTensorDescU`. -/
lemma stalkTensorDesc_germ (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
    (w : ↑((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf.obj (op U))) :
    (stalkTensorDesc A B x)
        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx)) w)
      = (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) w := by
  rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_stalkTensorDesc]

/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear d.2 comparison map** (stage (iii)). The additive comparison
map `stalkTensorDesc` is `R.stalk x`-linear for the stalk module structures of
`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`: linearity is checked on a germ `germ r` of a
scalar `r : R(U)` via the germ--scalar compatibility `germ_smul` and the section-level
scalar compatibility `stalkTensorDescU_smul`, after passing to a common neighbourhood.
This is the linear packaging the d.2 stalk--tensor isomorphism `stalkTensorIso` builds on. -/
noncomputable def stalkTensorLinearMap :
    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)] Tgt where
  toFun := ConcreteCategory.hom (stalkTensorDesc A B x)
  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
  map_smul' r ξ := by
    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
    obtain ⟨V, hxV, z, rfl⟩ :=
      TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x ξ
    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
    have hz : (ConcreteCategory.hom
          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf V x hxV)) z
        = (ConcreteCategory.hom
            (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf W x hxW))
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf.map iWV.op)) z) :=
      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf iWV x hxW z).symm
    rw [hr, hz, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul (Monoidal.tensorObj A B) x W hxW,
      stalkTensorDesc_germ, stalkTensorDescU_smul, ← stalkTensorDesc_germ]

end StalkTensor

    stalkTensorLinearMap A B x
        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx))
          (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b))
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) a ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk x)]
        (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx)) b :=
  stalkTensorDesc_germ_tmul A B x U hx a b

end StalkTensor

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (4, all axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):**
  - `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorDescU_smul` (line ~109) — section-level scalar
    compatibility of the per-neighbourhood descent.
  - `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorDesc_germ` (line ~177) — element form of
    `germ_stalkTensorDesc` (for a general section, not just a tmul).
  - `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorLinearMap` (line ~187) — **THE iter-234 convergence
    probe: stage (iii), the `R.stalk x`-linear repackaging of the forward comparison map.**
  - `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul` (line ~213) — germ-tmul
    characterisation of the linear map (cheap; for stage (v) / downstream).
- **Declarations blocked (0 attempted-and-failed):** none. Stopped at the *next* stage
  (iv, the reverse map), which is a large independent construction — see "Why I stopped".
- **Sorry count:** 0 → 0 (file is fully axiom-clean; `lake env lean` exit 0, no warnings).

## PROBE RESULT: `stalkTensorLinearMap` LANDED ✅ (axiom-clean)
The iter-234 binary convergence probe asked: *does `stalkTensorLinearMap` land this iter?*
**Yes.** Stage (iii) is complete. The carrier-duality obstacle flagged in PROGRESS
(CommRingCat `R(U)` scalar vs the RingCat-carrier module on `A(U) ⊗ B(U)`) was the real
work and is now **resolved** (see below). This distinguishes the d.2 lane decisively from
the old dual-route stall: a concrete, named obstacle was identified and closed, eliminating
the route's stated risk.

## `stalkTensorDescU_smul` (line ~109)
- **Statement:** `stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx (r • z) = germ_R r • stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx z`
  for `r : R(U)`, `z : (A ⊗ᵖ B)(U)`.
- **Approach:** `TensorProduct.induction_on z`.
  - `tmul a b`: `erw [stalkTensorDescU_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul, germ_smul A x U hx]`
    then `exact (TensorProduct.smul_tmul' _ _ _).symm`. **KEY:** the section smul
    `r • (a ⊗ₜ b)` is *defeq* to `(r • a) ⊗ₜ b`, so `erw [stalkTensorDescU_tmul]` matches
    `descU (r • (a⊗b))` directly through reducible defeq — no explicit `smul_tmul'` rewrite
    is needed (and `rw [smul_tmul']` FAILS on instance synthesis — do not use it).
  - `add p q hp hq`: `erw [smul_add]` (pulls the raw-`TensorProduct` smul via defeq), then
    `refine (map_add _ _ _).trans ?_; rw [hp, hq, ← smul_add]; exact congrArg _ (map_add _ p q).symm`.
  - `zero`: `simp only [presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj, CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj,
    Monoidal.tensorObj_obj, map_zero, smul_zero]; exact map_zero _` — the residual
    `descU (r • 0) = 0` closes because `r • 0` is **defeq** `0` (so `exact map_zero _` fires).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.
- **CARRIER-DUALITY GOTCHAS (load-bearing, record for any future stalk-tensor work):**
  1. `stalkTensorDescU_tmul` is stated with `⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)]` (CommRingCat coe) but the
     actual section tensor is `⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ _ _).obj (op U))]` (RingCat coe). These are
     defeq but NOT syntactic → use **`erw`**, never `rw`, for `stalkTensorDescU_tmul`.
  2. After `TensorProduct.induction_on`, `z` is re-presented as a raw `TensorProduct ↑S ..`
     (`S = (R ⋙ forget₂).obj (op U)`), whose smul lemmas (`smul_tmul'`, `smul_zero`,
     `smul_add`) want scalar type `↑S`, but the lemma's `r : ↑(R.obj (op U))` (CommRingCat).
     They are defeq but instance synthesis / `rw` keyed-matching FAILS. The working route is
     **defeq via `erw`** (tmul/add) or **defeq via `exact ... map_zero` / `smul_zero`** (zero).
  3. `map_add`/`map_zero` do NOT `rw`-match `ConcreteCategory.hom g (x+y)` / `... 0`, but DO
     work via `refine (map_add _ _ _).trans` / `exact map_zero _` (unification/term mode).
     The cocone proof of `stalkTensorDesc` already used `AddMonoidHom.map_add _ p q` term-form.

## `stalkTensorDesc_germ` (line ~177)
- **Approach:** `rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_stalkTensorDesc]` (mirrors the existing
  `stalkTensorDesc_germ_tmul`, generalised to an arbitrary section `w`).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## `stalkTensorLinearMap` (line ~187)
- **Statement:** `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x →ₗ[R.stalk x] (A.stalk x ⊗_{R.stalk x} B.stalk x)`,
  `toFun := ConcreteCategory.hom (stalkTensorDesc A B x)`.
- **Approach (mirrors d.1 `stalkLinearMap` map_smul', Vestigial.lean ~398–426):**
  `map_smul' r ξ`: `germ_exist` both `r = germ_R r₀` and `ξ = germ_{A⊗B} z`; pass to the
  common neighbourhood `W = U ⊓ V` via `germ_res_apply`; then
  `rw [hr, hz, ← germ_smul (tensorObj A B) x W hxW, stalkTensorDesc_germ,
       stalkTensorDescU_smul, ← stalkTensorDesc_germ]`.
  The chain: combine the two germs into `germ_W (r₀' • z')` (← germ_smul), descend
  (`stalkTensorDesc_germ`), pull the scalar (`stalkTensorDescU_smul`), re-ascend.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean. This is the stage-(iii) deliverable.

## Why I stopped
**Real progress: 4 axiom-clean declarations added** (named above), closing stage (iii) of
`lem:stalk_tensor_commutation` — the iteration's stated convergence probe. The hard part
was the carrier-duality, now fully diagnosed and solved (recipe above).

I stopped at the **stage (iv) reverse map** because it is a large independent construction,
not because a step was hard, and not at a sorry:
- The reverse map `A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x → (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x` is built by `TensorProduct.lift` of an
  `R_x`-bilinear map out of the **two** stalk colimits (`germ a, germ b ↦ germ_{U∩V}(a|⊗b|)`)
  — a *nested* `colimit.desc` with `R_x`-bilinearity and cocone-compatibility obligations
  (~150–250 LOC, blueprint stage (iv)).
- **No Mathlib shortcut exists:** searched `TensorProduct.directLimit` / `Module.DirectLimit`
  tensor-commutation — ABSENT. So there is no "tensor commutes with filtered colimit" lemma to
  cite; the descent must be built from `TopCat.Presheaf.stalk` (categorical colimit in
  `AddCommGrpCat`) by hand.
- Under **mathlib-build (no sorry pins)** a half-built reverse map is not committable, so the
  rational committable stopping point is stage (iii) complete. Stage (iv) is the natural unit
  of the *next* prover dispatch.

## Precise handoff — stages (iv)/(v)
1. **Stage (iv) reverse map** `stalkTensorRev : Tgt →ₗ[R.stalk x] (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x`.
   - Build the `R.stalk x`-bilinear `revBilin : A.stalk x →ₗ B.stalk x →ₗ (A⊗ᵖB).stalk x`
     by descending each factor: `revBilin (germ_U a) (germ_V b) := germ_{U⊓V}((a|)⊗(b|))`.
     Mechanise via two nested `colimit.desc`s (or `TopCat.Presheaf.stalk` germ-universal-property);
     well-definedness = filteredness of the neighbourhood system; `R_x`-bilinearity reduces to
     `germ_smul`. Then `stalkTensorRev := TensorProduct.lift revBilin`.
   - **Reuse the carrier-duality recipe above verbatim** — the same CommRingCat/RingCat coe
     and `erw`/defeq tactics will be needed when computing `germ_smul`/`tmul` at section level.
2. **Stage (v) bundle** `stalkTensorIso` (blueprint `\lean{PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorIso}`):
   - Forward = `stalkTensorLinearMap` (DONE), reverse = stage (iv).
   - `stalkTensorLinearMap ∘ rev = id` on `germ a ⊗ germ b` by `stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul`
     (DONE) + `TensorProduct.induction_on`.
   - `rev ∘ stalkTensorLinearMap = id` on `germ_{A⊗B}(a⊗b)` by the same characterisation; extend
     by `stalk_hom_ext` (germ joint-epi) + `TensorProduct.induction_on` on each section.

## Blueprint note (for plan/review — provers don't touch markers)
- `lem:stalk_tensor_commutation` §`sec:tensorobj_stalk_tensor` stage (iii) prose names
  `stalkTensorLinearMap`; it is now FORMALIZED and could carry its own `\lean{}` pin
  (e.g. a `lem:stalk_tensor_linear_map` block) — suggest the plan agent add it. The existing
  forward pin `lem:stalk_tensor_desc_forward` (`\lean{stalkTensorDesc}`) is unchanged.
- `stalkTensorLinearMap`, `stalkTensorDescU_smul`, `stalkTensorDesc_germ`,
  `stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul` are all ready for `\leanok` (handled by `sync_leanok`).

## Structural note (honoured)
Kept `StalkTensor.lean` import-minimal (`import Mathlib` only). Did NOT import `Vestigial`;
mirrored d.1 `stalkLinearMap`'s germ-representative pattern rather than importing it
(preserving the consumer-wiring direction Vestigial → StalkTensor).

Done. Here's a summary of this iteration's work on `StalkTensor.lean` (d.2 lane, stage iii).

## Result: the convergence probe landed ✅

The iter-234 binary probe asked *does `stalkTensorLinearMap` land this iter?* — **Yes.** Stage (iii) of `lem:stalk_tensor_commutation` is complete, with **4 new axiom-clean declarations** (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`; `lake env lean` exit 0, no warnings):

1. **`stalkTensorDescU_smul`** — section-level scalar compatibility of the per-neighbourhood descent.
2. **`stalkTensorDesc_germ`** — element form of the germ-factorisation.
3. **`stalkTensorLinearMap`** — *the deliverable*: the `R.stalk x`-linear repackaging `(A⊗ᵖB).stalk x →ₗ[R.stalk x] A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`.
4. **`stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul`** — germ-tmul characterisation (for stage v / downstream).

## The real work: the carrier-duality, now solved

The PROGRESS-flagged obstacle (CommRingCat scalar `r : R(U)` vs the RingCat-carrier module `↑S` on the section tensor `A(U)⊗B(U)`) was the genuine difficulty. The fix is a precise `erw`/defeq recipe (fully documented in the handoff + memory): the section smuls `r•(a⊗b)`, `r•0`, `r•(p+q)` are *defeq* to their pulled forms, so `erw [stalkTensorDescU_tmul]` / `exact map_zero _` / `erw [smul_add]` fire — whereas `rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']`, `map_add`/`map_zero` on `ConcreteCategory.hom` all **fail** on instance/keyed-matching. This closes the route's stated risk and distinguishes the d.2 lane decisively from the old dual-route stall (a named obstacle was identified and eliminated).

## Why I stopped here

The next step — **stage (iv), the reverse map** `A_x⊗_{R_x}B_x → (A⊗ᵖB).stalk x` — is a large, independent nested `colimit.desc` construction (~150–250 LOC). I verified there is **no Mathlib shortcut** (`TensorProduct.directLimit` / `Module.DirectLimit` tensor-commutation is absent), and under mathlib-build (no sorry) a half-built reverse map isn't committable. So the rational committable stopping point is stage (iii) complete; stage (iv) is the natural next-dispatch unit. The handoff (`task_results/StalkTensor.lean.md`) gives the precise construction recipe for stages (iv) and (v), reusing the carrier-duality recipe.
